7.reason: [This condition is incorrect: it accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i>0 and i is not at the last character, leading to out-of-bounds access when '-' is at the start or end of string. Also, the logic should first verify that both surrounding characters are of the same valid type before comparing their values.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  // This condition is incorrect: it should check that both characters are of the same type and that s[i-1] < s[i+1], but it accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without verifying i > 0 and i < length-1, leading to out-of-bounds access when '-' is at the start or end.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i > 0 and i < length-1, leading to out-of-bounds access when '-' is at the start or end of the string.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  // This condition is incorrect: it should check that both characters are of the same type and that s[i-1] < s[i+1], but accessing s[i-1] when i=0 causes undefined behavior (out-of-bounds read).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is incorrect: it should check that both characters are of the same type and that s[i-1] < s[i+1], but it accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without verifying i > 0 and i < length-1, leading to out-of-bounds access when '-' is at the start or end. Also, the condition s[i - 1] < s[i + 1] - 1 excludes cases like 'a-b' where only one character should be inserted.]
